content:
been trying different models for frontend work and landed on something unexpected this week. minimax just open sourced m3, 428b parameter moe model with 23b active at inference. figured id give it a real test on my actual project.
fed it my entire next.js repo as context. around 180k tokens worth of components, hooks, utils, tailwind config, the works. then gave it figma screenshots and asked it to build new components that match the existing design system.
couple things stood out. it actually picked up on the design patterns already in the codebase. like it noticed i was using a specific compound component pattern for forms and followed that without me having to spell it out. most models i try either ignore the existing patterns or need explicit instructions about them.
the multimodal part surprised me. it read figma screenshots and translated spacing, color tokens and layout structure pretty accurately. still needed manual tweaks on responsive breakpoints but the first pass was closer than usual.
where it fell short was interactive stuff. a drag and drop reorder component came back functional but it used raw usestate for everything when my codebase uses zustand stores. had to refactor the whole state layer. for static or lightly interactive components though it was solid.
curious if other frontend devs have tried this or similar open weights models on real project context.

  submitted by   /u/Right_Hunt6790
[link]   [comments]
